rulings.Case Title: ELISA RECODER VS. ADECCO USA, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 19, 2026, line 2. FINAL DISTRIBUTION HEARING (PAGA). Continued to May 27, 2026, because the check cashing deadline is April 15, 2026. Plaintiff shall file a declaration from the settlement administrator detailing the distribution by May 13, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: HEATHER YARBROUGH VS. ELSIE ECLEVIA CURAMENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Deem Admitted Request For Admission (Set Two) To Defendant Elsie Eclevia Curameng
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 19, 2026, line 8. PLAINTIFF HEATHER YARBROUGH AS TRUSTEE'S Motion To Deem Admitted Request For Admission (Set Two) To Defendant Elsie Eclevia Curameng. Plaintiff Heather Yarbrough's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in her Requests for Admission, Set Two, is granted. Defendant Elsie Eclevia Curameng failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280, subd. (a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set Two, is deemed admitted by this defendant. (Id., sec. 2033.280, subds. (b), (c).) Yarbrough does not seek sanctions, but they are mandatory in this instance. (Id., sec. 2033.280, subd. (c).) The court orders Curameng to pay $100 to Yarbrough within 30 days of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: DARIO CASTRO MENDEZ VS. MARIELA STEPHANIE MEDINA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 19, 2026, line 12. PLAINTIFF DARIO MENDEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Plaintiff Dario Mendez moves to quash deposition subpoenas for the production of business records issued to MedicWest Ambulance, San Francisco Emergency Medical Association, and Health Diagnostic of CA, on grounds of privacy and overbreadth. The motion is denied, and the court orders Plaintiff to pay $480 in sanctions to Defendant by March 19, 2026. Plaintiff has put at issue the medical conditions he claims were caused by the accident in this case, and has thereby waived his privacy and physician-patient confidentiality rights as to those conditions. (Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 842.) Defendant presents evidence that the subpoenas were issued to those treaters Plaintiff identified as treating him after the accident. Plaintiff's claims of injury are broad, and the subpoenas seek records only from the prior 10 yeras. Plaintiff raises the issue that psychiatric records may be part of the records requested, but offers no evidentiary support for that assertion, and the court therefore need not decide whether Plaintiff has put his mental health records at issue with his claim that he suffered shock and injury to his nervous system or great mental pain and suffering. The court concludes that Plaintiff's motion lacked substantial justification and orders Plaintiff to pay $480 in sanctions to Defendant. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DARIO CASTRO MENDEZ VS. MARIELA STEPHANIE MEDINA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 19, 2026, line 12. PLAINTIFF DARIO MENDEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Off calendar as untimely filed and served. See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1008, subd. (b). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KJELL SPANGBERG ET AL VS. GARY BAUER ET AL
